title: realloc Semantics and Transactional Growth · Stakeholder Native Systems Mission
summary: A public-interest document archive. The archive needs a growing C index with opaque ownership, transactional updates, partial-initialization cleanup, bounded strings, allocation failure injection, and preserved old state. Planned artifact: Deliver Failure-Atomic Document Index with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Hide representation behind incomplete types and constructor, observer, mutation, and destructor contracts.
Inject matched allocate, resize, and release operations with context and failure semantics.
Represent which subresources are initialized so cleanup is valid after every acquisition point.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Project Opaque Structure Evolution
steps.instruction:
project case — A project native-systems team handles memory case 9908. Produce bounded C evidence to grow capacity without losing the old allocation on failure; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.

At Test an acceptance boundary · Project Growth Factor Budget, trace this concrete input and predict size arithmetic, pointer range, lifetime transition, allocation result, cleanup order, printed output, and native evidence still missing before reviewing it: A project native-systems team handles memory case 5956. Produce bounded C evidence to grow capacity without losing the old allocation on failure; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose bounded geometric growth from amortized cost, overflow, maximum capacity, and memory-pressure evidence.

For Test an acceptance boundary · Project Growth Factor Budget, challenge the documented misconception with changed-size, failure-injection, printed invariant, compiler, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, ABI, concurrency, or production-transfer evidence: Doubling capacity is universally optimal and cannot overflow.

stimulus.title: Growth Factor Budget failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Doubling capacity is universally optimal and cannot overflow.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose bounded geometric growth from amortized cost, overflow, maximum capacity, and memory-pressure evidence.

options.text: Keep the plausible Growth Factor Budget output and hide the violated amortized vector with failure invariants assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Growth Factor Budget.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Growth Factor Budget case, omit size, bounds, lifetime, owner, allocation failure, cleanup, invariant output, and native transfer gates, then call the browser result production-safe. This does not satisfy Growth Factor Budget for amortized vector with failure invariants.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Growth Factor Budget condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Growth Factor Budget
steps.instruction:
Write the project evidence note for Growth Factor Budget: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A project native-systems team handles memory case 7575. Produce bounded C evidence to grow capacity without losing the old allocation on failure; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose bounded geometric growth from amortized cost, overflow, maximum capacity, and memory-pressure evidence.

Evidence target: Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Growth Factor Budget.
steps.why: Retrieving Growth Factor Budget through the concrete amortized vector with failure invariants case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Growth Factor Budget, keep Growth Factor Budget connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose bounded geometric growth from amortized cost, overflow, maximum capacity, and memory-pressure evidence.
